data.title: VeriSign 3Q Profit, Revenue Up on Domain-Name Registration Growth
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

VeriSign posted higher profit and revenue in its latest quarter as domain-name registrations rose.

The domain-name-registry provider on Thursday posted a profit of $212.8 million, or $2.27 a share, compared to $201.3 million, or $2.07 a share, a year earlier.

Revenue rose 7.3% to $419.1 million.

VeriSign ended the quarter with domain-name registrations up 1.4% from a year ago to 171.9 million.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Sallie Mae Swings to 3Q Profit, Raises FY Outlook
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

SLM, the company behind Sallie Mae, swung to a profit in the third quarter and raised its full-year guidance as its charge-offs and credit-loss provision shrunk.

The student loan lender on Thursday posted a profit of $135.9 million, or 63 cents a share, in the quarter ended Sept. 30, compared with a loss of $45.2 million, or 23 cents a share, a year earlier. Analysts polled by FactSet expected a profit of 79 cents a share.

Net interest income was $373 million, missing the $382.8 million Wall Street expected. Average loans outstanding increased 9% to $22.3 billion.

The company had a lower provision for credit losses and a smaller percentage of its average loans in repayment were charge-offs. Delinquencies as a share of loans ticked up slightly.

Sallie Mae raised its full-year outlook for earnings per share to $3.20 to $3.30, up 20 cents a share from its previous range.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: M-tron Industries Files 8K - Other Events >MPTI
data.description:
M-tron Industries Inc. (MPTI) filed a Form 8K - Other Events - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on October 23, 2025.

On October 23, 2025, M-tron Industries, Inc. (the "Company") issued a press release announcing that the warrants to purchase shares of the Company's common stock, par value $0.01 per share (the "Common Stock"), granted on April 25, 2025 (the "Warrants") are now exercisable.

Pursuant to the terms of that certain Warrant Agreement, dated April 25, 2025 (the "Warrant Agreement"), by and among the Company, Computershare Inc. ("Computershare"), and Computershare Trust Company, N.A. (together with Computershare, the "Warrant Agent"), the Warrants are exercisable at the earlier of (i) thirty (30) days prior to April 25, 2028 and (ii) such date that the average volume weighted average price ("VWAP") of the Common Stock is greater than or equal to $52.00 per share for the prior thirty (30) consecutive trading day period (such date, the "Trigger"); provided, however, that should the Trigger occur, the Warrants must be exercised within thirty (30) days of the Company's notification pursuant to the Warrant Agreement that the Trigger has occurred (the "Trigger Expiration Date"), unless extended in the Company's discretion.

The Trigger was achieved on October 20, 2025. The Company has elected to extend the warrant exercise period to 5:00 p.m. on Thursday December 11, 2025 (the "Modified Trigger Expiration Date"). The Warrants shall cease to be exercisable and shall terminate and become void, and all rights thereunder and under the Warrant Agreement shall cease, at the close of business on the Modified Trigger Expiration Date.

Additionally, Warrant holders who exercise their Warrants in full are also entitled to an over-subscription privilege, whereby such Warrant holder may also choose to subscribe for any or all of the shares issuable pursuant to any unexercised Warrants on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in the Warrant Agreement.

All exercise notices and payments (including with respect to any exercise of a Warrant holder's over-subscription privilege) must be received by the Warrant Agent no later than the Modified Trigger Expiration Date. Holders in street name should contact their broker, bank, or other intermediary for information on how to exercise their Warrants (including pursuant to any exercise of the over-subscription privilege).

A copy of the press release is attached as Exhibit 99.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and is incorporated by reference to this Item 8.01.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1902314/000143774925031645/mpti20251015_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1902314/000143774925031645/0001437749-25-031645-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: How Crocs Outsmarted Starbucks in China's $7 Trillion Consumer War
data.description:
Crocs Inc. has emerged as one of the most unexpected winners in China's consumer market, transforming from a niche boating clog into a cultural statement among young Chinese shoppers. After years of uneven performance, the company is now benefiting from what analysts describe as emotional consumption small, joy-driven purchases that have become popular in a slowing economy. In its most recent quarter, Crocs reported China sales up more than 30%, while revenue in North America declined 6.5%, signaling a sharp divergence in global consumer behavior. Analysts note that Crocs' momentum in China could continue as the country's $7 trillion retail market evolves toward experience-based spending and self-expression.

The company's turnaround in China has been fueled by deep localization. Crocs appointed Chinese actress Yang Mi as brand ambassador and integrated local digital trends into its marketing. The ability to customize clogs with colorful charms ranging from cartoon figures to food icons has given the brand a strong foothold with Gen Z consumers. Market observers point out that Crocs' appeal now lies in its perceived ugliness, which has become a badge of individuality among Chinese youth. This shift from global uniformity to local authenticity has allowed Crocs to resonate more strongly than competitors who continue to rely on Western-centric brand playbooks.

Starbucks and LVMH (LVMHF) offer contrasting examples. Starbucks, once dominant in China's coffee scene, has been overtaken by local upstart Luckin Coffee, while luxury giant LVMH has seen sales in its Greater China region flatten after last year's post-COVID rebound. LVMH's latest Shanghai pop-up, The Louis, may have generated attention, but analysts view it as a defensive move to reconnect with younger consumers seeking experiences over status. Crocs' success suggests that in China's shifting consumption landscape, emotional connection and cultural fluency could now matter more than legacy or luxury a lesson global brands are being forced to learn in real time.

data.title: The end of October often rewards stock investors with this profitable trade
data.description:
By Lawrence G. McMillan

Many institutional investors close their fiscal year on Oct. 31 and buy stocks at that time with excess cash

A longstanding seasonal trade that has had excellent results over the years occurs at the end of October.

The trade doesn't work every year, but the setup is simple enough: Buy "the market" at the close of trading on Oct. 27 and exit the trade at the close of trading on Nov. 2 (since Nov. 2 is a Sunday this year, the trade would be exited at the close of trading on Nov. 3). We have traded this system for over 40 years, and it became evident that it works best when there is a large selloff sometime during October.

The reason that this seasonal trade "works" is that many institutional investors end their fiscal year on Oct. 31 and often buy at that time, especially if there is "too much" cash on their books. The only reason there would be excess cash would be that the market had fallen sharply sometime before that and they sold some stocks. By the end of the month they would be looking to get rid of that cash at the end of the fiscal year, and so be "properly" invested in stocks.

Specifically, we only take this trade if there has been at least a 3.2% decline - using closing prices - sometime during the month of October. This year, the highest close for SPX was 6,735 on Oct. 9. The next day it fell 2.7%, but it has rallied since. So there has not been a decline of 3.2% again this year. We will not take this seasonal trade if that continues to be the case.

But here is the contingent trade:

If SPX closes at 6,519 or lower on any day prior to and including Oct. 27, then at the close of trading on Oct. 27, enter this trade:

Buy 1 SPY (Nov. 14) at-the-money call and sell 1 SPY (Nov. 14) call with a striking price 15 points higher.

If the trade is entered, then exit the entire position at the close of trading on Nov. 3.

The S&P 500 index SPX has been more volatile since it experienced the large drop on Oct. 10, but it has recovered a great deal of those losses, and the SPX chart remains bullish. SPX has tested support in the 6,500-6,550 area five times since the middle of September, and it has held every time. So, regardless of what other indicators might be showing (and some are bearish), the SPX chart is the most important indicator at this time. A close below 6,500 would change the picture from bullish to bearish.

A McMillan volatility band (MVB) sell signal was confirmed on Oct. 10, when SPX fell below 6,680. But once again, there has not been any follow-through to that signal. It will remain in effect until it reaches either of the +/-4<SIGMA> bands, but those have spread far apart due to the increase in realized volatility that has taken place.

Equity-only put-call ratios are on sell signals as well, as they continue to rise from the recent lows. As long as these ratios are trending higher, that is a sell signal for stocks. Moreover, these sell signals are arising from deeply overbought conditions (i.e., the ratios were at or near the lows on their charts when they rolled over and began to rise). Such signals are normally the strongest ones.

Market breadth has been poor throughout the strong SPX rally since April. That is a nagging condition that some consider to be a negative divergence in the stock market. I'm not going that far, but one would normally expect to see the breadth oscillators at much higher levels after such a strong period for SPX. Regardless, the breadth oscillators are on buy signals at this time.

Cumulative volume breadth (CVB) is perhaps a better measure of how breadth is behaving in this market. It is the running daily sum of the volume on advancing stocks minus the volume on declining stocks. This measure has been strong and has kept pace with SPX on the upside. In fact, CVB made a new all-time high on both Oct. 15 and 16.

In general, volatility - both realized and implied - has been rising, and that is a negative for stocks. However, the big spike in volatility occurred last week (culminating with the Cboe Volatility Index VIX near 29 on Oct. 17 and then falling back sharply). So perhaps the brief spate of an explosion in volatility was just a short-lived phenomenon. In any case, the 20-day historical volatility of SPX (HV20) has continued to rise and is now at 14%. Since it is rising, it remains on a sell signal that occurred two weeks ago. If it were to fall back to 10% or lower, that would stop out the sell signal.

VIX threw a scare into the market with its behavior leading up to the sharp spike peak at 29. Not only did VIX jump sharply, but the associated trading instruments - futures and options - were in disarray as well. In the end, though, VIX pulled back sharply and created two new buy signals in doing so.

The first was a new "spike peak" buy signal on Oct. 17 as VIX closed at 20.78 after having been as high as 28.99 earlier in the day. That buy signal is marked with a green "B" on the accompanying VIX chart. This was soon followed by a new "trend of VIX" buy signal, when VIX continued to decline further and descended back below its 200-day moving average. This new buy signal is marked with a pink "B" on the chart. Note that there was a similar trend of VIX buy signal back in June, and that was a strong signal, which had only recently been stopped out.

When VIX shot higher, it caused a severe disturbance in the construct of volatility derivatives. The term structures of the VIX futures flattened out and even inverted sharply in the front end of the curve. At the close of trading on Oct. 16, the front-month October futures were trading 1.60 higher than the second-month November futures. By the morning of the next day, that differential had increased to over two points.

When the inversion is that great, it is usually a dire warning for stocks. But this time the damage to stocks was contained overnight, and everything pretty much went back to normal. Frankly, such a quick recovery has never really happened before, but I suppose it is just a further reflection on the short-term nature of markets today. In any case, the construct has returned to a bullish state for stocks, as the term structures slope upwards, and the VIX futures are trading at a premium to VIX (November VIX futures are now the front month, since October futures have expired).

In summary, the increase in volatility is worrisome, but as long as SPX remains above 6,500, there really isn't anything to worry about. Most of our indicators remain bullish, especially since there have been two recent buy signals added to the mix. Continue to roll deeply in-the-money options.

New recommendation: VIX 'spike peak' buy signal

On Oct. 13, VIX closed more than three points below its high of Oct. 10 (that peak was 22.44). This confirmed a new "spike peak" buy signal for the stock market. But just a few days later, on Oct. 16, VIX blasted to a higher high, stopping out that previous buy signal. That set up the possibility of another "spike peak" buy signal, which occurred on Oct. 17 when VIX traded up to 28.99 but closed much lower, at 20.78. That buy signal is still in effect, so:

Buy 1 SPY SPY (Nov. 28) at-the-money call and sell 1 SPY (Nov. 28) call with a striking price 15 points higher.

Stop out if VIX closes above 28.99 on any day. Otherwise, we will hold for 22 trading days. Roll up if SPY trades at the higher strike.

New recommendation: Boston Scientific (BSX)

There has been a new weighted put-call ratio buy signal in Boston Scientific Corp. (BSX).

Buy 2 BSX (Dec. 19) 100 calls at a price of 6.50 or less.

We will hold these calls as long as the weighted put-call ratio for BSX remains on a buy signal.

Follow-up action:

All stops are mental closing stops unless otherwise noted.

We are using a standard rolling procedure for our SPY spreads: In any vertical bull or bear spread, if the underlying hits the short strike, then roll the entire spread. That would be roll up in the case of a call bull spread or roll down in the case of a bear put spread. Stay in the same expiration and keep the distance between the strikes the same unless otherwise instructed.

Also, for outright long options, roll if they become 8 points in-the-money.

Long 2 APH (APH) (Nov. 21) 130 calls: The stock briefly jumped higher on Amphenol Corp.'s Oct. 22 earnings report but quickly gave back those gains. Use a trailing closing stop at 124 for these calls.

Long 1 TSEM (TSEM) (Nov. 17) 75 call and short 1 TSEM (Nov. 21) 85 call: Set a trailing, close stop at 69 for this spread.

Long 1 SPY SPY (Oct. 31) 672 call and sell 1 SPY (Oct. 31) 687 call: We will hold until new lows outnumber new highs on two consecutive days on the New York Stock Exchange.

Long 4 ATAI (ATAI) (Nov. 21) 6 calls: Use a trailing closing stop at 5.20 for these calls.

Long 1 GLD (Nov. 21) 400 call and short 1 GLD (Nov. 21) 415 call: This spread was rolled up twice this past week. Once, when SPDR Gold Shares GLD traded at 385 on Oct. 15, and then again when it traded at 400 on Oct. 20. Raise the closing, trailing stop to 371 for this spread.

Long 2 NKE (Nov. 21) 72.5 puts: We will hold these as long as the weighted put-call ratio for Nike Inc. (NKE) is on a sell signal.

Long 6 BITF (BITF) (Nov. 21) 5.5 calls: Sell these calls now, since Bitfarms Ltd.'s stock has pulled back too far.

Long 0 ASTS (Nov. 21) 100 call and short 0 ASTS (Nov. 21) 120 call: This spread was stopped out when AST SpaceMobile Inc. (ASTS) closed below 72 on Oct. 22.

Long 10 CANE CANE (Nov. 21) 10 calls: We will hold as long as the put-call ratio for sugar futures remains on a buy signal.

Long 1 SPY (Nov. 21) 665 put and short 1 SPY (Nov. 21) 615 put: This is the position based on the MVB sell signal. It was confirmed on Oct. 13, when SPX fell below 6,680. Continue to hold until SPX touches either of the +/-4<SIGMA> bands.

Gentherm Inc SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Gentherm Inc, a global leader in thermal management technologies, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting steady growth in product revenues and strategic business initiatives aimed at optimizing global manufacturing capacity.

Financial Highlights

Gentherm Inc reported the following key financial metrics for the third quarter of 2025:

  • Product Revenues: $386.87 million, marking a 4.1% increase compared to the same period last year. This growth was driven by favorable automotive volumes and positive foreign currency impacts.
  • Gross Margin: $95.15 million, representing 24.6% of product revenues, a slight increase from $94.87 million in the prior year period.
  • Operating Income: $23.86 million, a decrease of 26.2% from the previous year, primarily due to higher operating expenses.
  • Net Income: $14.95 million, a slight decrease from $15.97 million in the prior year, reflecting increased costs and expenses.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $0.49, compared to $0.51 in the previous year, reflecting the decrease in net income.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.49, consistent with the basic earnings per share, compared to $0.51 in the previous year.

Business Highlights

Gentherm Inc's business performance for the third quarter of 2025 includes the following key highlights:

  • Revenue Segments: The Automotive segment reported product revenues of $374.2 million, with Climate Control Seats contributing $201.3 million, Lumbar and Massage Comfort Solutions $55.8 million, and Climate Control Interiors $52.6 million. The Medical segment reported revenues of $12.7 million.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States remains the largest market with revenues of $140.0 million. China and Germany followed with $63.7 million and $30.3 million, respectively. Notably, Mexico showed significant growth with revenues increasing to $18.3 million from $10.8 million in the prior year period.
  • Sales Units: Global light vehicle production in key markets increased by 5.4%, with notable growth in Greater China at 9.7%.
  • New Business Awards: During the third quarter, Gentherm secured automotive new business awards totaling $745 million, indicating strong future revenue potential.
  • Future Outlook: The S&P Global Mobility forecast anticipates a 1.9% increase in light vehicle production volumes in key markets for the full year 2025, suggesting a positive outlook for Gentherm's automotive segment.
  • Operational Challenges: The company continues to face challenges from global trade tensions and tariffs, although its regional manufacturing strategy helps mitigate some impacts. Inflationary pressures and supply chain disruptions remain concerns.
  • Restructuring Plans: Gentherm is executing several restructuring plans, including the 2025 Manufacturing Footprint Plan, the 2025 EMEA Plan, and the 2025 Asia Plan, aimed at optimizing global manufacturing capacity and reducing costs.

SEC Filing: Gentherm Inc [ THRM ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: CBRE GROUP, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CBRE Group, Inc., a global leader in real estate services and investment, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational advancements, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic initiatives in the real estate market.

Financial Highlights

CBRE Group, Inc. reported impressive financial results for the three months ended September 30, 2025, showcasing substantial growth across key metrics:

  • Revenue: $10,258 million, up from $9,036 million in the same period of 2024, driven by growth across various segments.
  • Operating Income: $481 million, an increase from $368 million in the same period of 2024, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $396 million, compared to $245 million in the same period of 2024, highlighting strong financial performance.
  • Net Income Attributable to CBRE Group, Inc.: $363 million, up from $225 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Basic Income Per Share: $1.22, compared to $0.73 in the same period of 2024, reflecting higher earnings per share.
  • Diluted Income Per Share: $1.21, compared to $0.73 in the same period of 2024, indicating increased profitability per share.

Business Highlights

CBRE Group, Inc. also reported significant operational achievements and strategic initiatives during the third quarter of 2025:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue across various segments, including Advisory Services, Global Workplace Solutions, Building Operations & Experience, Project Management, and Real Estate Investments. Each segment contributed to the overall revenue growth, with specific emphasis on the integration of Industrious into the Building Operations & Experience segment.
  • Geographical Performance: The company experienced varied performance across different regions, with notable growth in the European and Asian markets. The integration of Turner & Townsend's project management business further strengthened the company's presence in these regions.
  • New Product Launches: The acquisition of Industrious expanded the company's offerings in flexible workplace solutions, enhancing its Building Operations & Experience segment. This acquisition is expected to drive future growth by leveraging synergies in the flexible workspace market.
  • New Production Launches: The company completed the combination of its project management business with Turner & Townsend, increasing its ownership to 70% in the combined entity. This strategic move is aimed at expanding the company's project management capabilities and market reach.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in the Building Operations & Experience segment, driven by the full integration of Industrious and the expansion of flexible workplace solutions. Additionally, the company expects to leverage its increased stake in the Turner & Townsend project management business to capture more market opportunities.

SEC Filing: CBRE GROUP, INC. [ CBRE ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: Gold Royalty's Q3 revenue jumps on improved gold prices

  • Company expects to be nearly debt-free by end of 2026

Outlook

  • Company anticipates record-level cash flows in 2025 due to higher gold prices

data.title: Boston Beer Boosts FY Profit Outlook, Sees Lower Tariff Risk
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Boston Beer raised its profit guidance for the year as it sees a milder tariff headwind.

The brewer, which owns the Samuel Adams and Truly brands, now forecasts earnings per share of $7.80 to $9.80 for the year, up from its prior outlook of $6.72 to $9.54. Both ranges include added tariff costs.

The company now expects tariffs to add about $9 million to $13 million in costs for the year, down from its prior view of $15 million to $20 million.

The updated guidance comes after the company logged lower revenue in its latest quarter as depletions and shipments fell.

Boston Beer posted a profit of $46.2 million, or $4.25 a share, for the third quarter, compared with $33.5 million, or $2.86 a share, a year earlier. The year-ago quarter included a non-cash brand impairment charge of $29.1 million.

Analysts expected earnings of $3.33 a share, according to FactSet.

Net revenue, which excludes excise taxes, slid 11% to $537.5 million. Analysts expected $542.1 million.

Depletions, a key metric that tracks sales by distributors to retailers and is considered a proxy for demand, declined 3% as volumes were pressured across the beer industry, Chief Executive Officer Jim Koch said.

Koch, who returned as CEO in August, said the company is encouraged by positive reactions to its Sun Cruiser brand innovation and a second consecutive quarter of growth for its Angry Orchard business.

"We are continuing to invest in advertising and innovation across our portfolio of brands to grow market share and position ourselves well for when the consumer environment improves," Koch said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Correction to Thermo Fisher Article on Oct. 22
data.description: "Thermo Scientific 3Q Revenue Rises, Boosted by Life Sciences Business" published at 6:28 a.m. ET on Oct. 22, 2025 misstated the name of the company in the headline and first paragraph as Thermo Scientific instead of Thermo Fisher.

data.title: Applied Materials to Cut 4% of Global Workforce
data.description:
By Josée Rose

Applied Materials is cutting about 4% of its global workforce, effective immediately.

The semiconductor company said it is taking one coordinated workforce action as "automation, digitalization and geographic shifts are redefining our workforce needs and skill requirements," according to a letter to employees, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday.

The company has started notifying employees as of Oct. 23, the letter said.

Applied Materials expects to incur charges of about $160 million to $180 million related to the terminations, and will recognize most of that cost in the fourth quarter. The plan should be completed in the fiscal-first quarter of 2026.

The company plans to continue its focus on "building high-velocity, high-productivity teams, adopting new technologies and simplifying organizational structures" and wants to be "better positioned to scale effectively," according to the letter.

Applied Materials had 36,100 regular full-time employees as of July 27, according to its latest earnings release from August.

Write to Josée Rose at josee.rose@wsj.com

data.title: Western Union Co Q3 Profit Decreases, But Beats Estimates
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Western Union Co (WU) reported earnings for third quarter that Decreased from the same period last year but beat the Street estimates.

The company's bottom line came in at $139.6 million, or $0.43 per share. This compares with $264.8 million, or $0.78 per share, last year.

Excluding items, Western Union Co reported adjusted earnings of $151.2 million or $0.47 per share for the period.

Analysts on average had expected the company to earn $0.43 per share. Analysts' estimates typically exclude special items.

The company's revenue for the period fell 0.4% to $1.032 billion from $1.036 billion last year.

Western Union Co earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $139.6 Mln. vs. $264.8 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $0.43 vs. $0.78 last year.

-Revenue: $1.032 Bln vs. $1.036 Bln last year.

<b>-Guidance</b>:

Full year EPS guidance: $1.65 - $1.75

Full year revenue guidance: $4.085 - $4.185 Bln

data.title: CEO of eXoZymes to Present at ThinkEquity's Annual Investor Conference
data.description:
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA / ACCESS Newswire / October 23, 2025 / Today, eXoZymes Inc. ("eXoZymes") - a pioneer of AI-engineered enzymes that can transform sustainable feedstock into nutraceuticals, medicines, and other essential chemicals - announced that CEO, Michael Heltzen, will present at the 2025 ThinkEquity Conference on October 30, 2025, at the Mandarin Oriental Hotel in New York City.

CEO of eXoZymes, Michael Heltzen, states, "Most investors already know that - compared to the number of dollars invested - synthetic biology unfortunately has very little to show for it because it never scaled commercially. That's why we have introduced the next generation of biomanufacturing - a groundbreaking new way of making chemicals by using AI to convert enzymes to function cell-free in a bioreactor. Our initial focus with this paradigm shifting platform is natural products with the potential to become pharmaceuticals. Leading this strategy with a natural product called NCT which is showing very promising results in improving metabolic health."

eXoZymes has developed a biomanufacturing platform that - as a historic first - offers the tools and insights to design, engineer, control and optimize nature's own natural processes to produce chemical compounds, enabling the company's partners to upgrade traditional petro-chemical production methods - or natural extraction - with a new commercially scalable, sustainable, and eco-friendly alternative: exozymes.

Heltzen continues, "By using the ingenuity of biology, coupled with AI, and combining it with the scalability of chemical engineering - we have solved the scaling bottleneck of synthetic biology. As such, we believe eXoZymes is positioned to lead the 4th industrial revolution at the intersection of biology and technology, which we're excited to detail to investors at ThinkEquity's conference next week!"

Organized by ThinkEquity LLC, the conference will showcase more than 85 carefully selected companies across biotechnology, artificial intelligence, crypto, mining, and other high-growth sectors. Over 750 institutional and high-net-worth investors are expected to attend.

Michael Heltzen will be presenting eXoZymes to investors on Thursday, October 30, at 8:00-8:30 AM in the Lotus Suite East at the Mandarin Oriental Hotel.

About ThinkEquity LLC

ThinkEquity is a boutique investment bank created by experienced professionals that have worked together for over a decade, collectively financing over $50 billion of public and private capital raises, restructurings, and mergers and acquisitions.

For more information about ThinkEquity, visit https://www.think-equity.com

About eXoZymes

Founded in 2019, the company has developed a biomanufacturing platform that - as a historic first - offers the tools and insights to design, engineer, control and optimize nature's own natural processes to produce chemical compounds, enabling the company's partners to upgrade traditional petro-chemical production methods with a new commercially scalable, sustainable, and eco-friendly alternative: exozymes.

Exozymes are advanced enzymes enhanced through AI and bioengineering to thrive in a bioreactor without using living cells. Exozymes can replace toxic petrochemical processes and inefficient biochemical extraction with sustainable and scalable biosolutions that transform biomass into essential chemicals, medicines, and biofuels.

By freeing enzyme-driven chemical reactions from the limitations imposed by cells, exozyme biosolutions eliminate the scaling bottleneck that has hampered commercial success in the synthetic biology (SynBio) space, making exozymes the next generation of biomanufacturing.

While the company, eXoZymes Inc., has introduced "exozymes" as a scientific concept, they are not trademarking the concept, as they view it as a new nomenclature for wide adoption for this next generation of biomanufacturing that eXoZymes aims to pioneer and be the market leader of.

Learn more on exozymes.com

eXoZymes Safe Harbor

This press release includes forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Forward-looking statements, which are based on certain assumptions and describe the company's future plans, strategies and expectations, can generally be identified by the use of forward-looking terms such as "believe," "expect," "may," "will," "should," "would," "could," "seek," "intend," "plan," "goal," "project," "estimate," "anticipate," "strategy," "future," "likely," "potential," or other comparable terms, although not all forward-looking statements contain these identifying words. You should carefully consider the risks and uncertainties described in the "Risk Factors" section of eXoZymes' quarterly reports on Form 10-Q, annual reports on Form 10-K, and other documents filed by eXoZymes from time to time by the company with the Securities and Exchange Commission. data.title: Anthropic to use 1 million Google Cloud TPU chips to train its Claude chatbot
data.description:
Anthropic said on Thursday it Claude models would be trained using up to one million of Google Cloud's tensor processing units, worth tens of billions of dollars.

The deal underscores the immense computing demands of AI firms for training, deployment and continuous inference in generative AI, as they race to launch new products and features to stay ahead in a fiercely competitive market.

To secure infrastructure swiftly, developers have been rushing into multi-billion dollar agreements .

Alphabet's Google, also a backer of Anthropic, will provide additional cloud computing services to the startup behind the Claude chatbot.

data.title: VERISIGN INC/CA SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Verisign Inc., a global leader in domain name registry services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational efficiency, driven by increased domain name registrations and price adjustments for .com and .net domains. The company continues to demonstrate robust performance across various metrics, reflecting its strong market position and strategic initiatives.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $419.1 million, a 7% increase compared to the same period in 2024, driven by .com and .net price increases and an expanded domain name base.
  • Operating Income: $284.3 million, representing a 6% increase from the same period in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $212.8 million, up from $201.3 million in the same period in 2024, indicating strong profitability.
  • Basic Earnings per Share (EPS): $2.28, compared to $2.07 in the same period in 2024, reflecting higher net income.
  • Diluted EPS: $2.27, compared to $2.07 in the same period in 2024, showing improved earnings performance.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company experienced revenue growth across all regions during the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025. The majority of growth was generated from registrars based in the U.S. and EMEA. U.S. revenues increased by 6% and 5% for the three and nine months, respectively, while EMEA saw a 13% and 12% increase.
  • Sales Units: As of September 30, 2025, Verisign had 171.9 million .com and .net registrations in the domain name base, representing a 1.4% increase from the previous year. During the three months ended September 30, 2025, 10.6 million new domain name registrations were processed, up from 9.3 million in the same period in 2024.
  • Renewal Rates: The final .com and .net renewal rate for the second quarter of 2025 was 75.5%, an improvement from 72.7% in the second quarter of 2024, indicating stronger customer retention.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company reported an increase in operating income by 6% and 5% for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: Verisign plans to continue paying a quarterly cash dividend, subject to market conditions and board approval, and has a significant amount remaining for future share repurchases under its current authorization.

SEC Filing: VERISIGN INC/CA [ VRSN ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: SLM Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
SLM Corp, a leading provider of private education loans, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its strategic focus on maximizing profitability and growth within its core private student loan business.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $658 million, increased by $6 million or 1% compared to the same period last year, driven by a $1.8 billion increase in average Private Education Loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $373 million, increased by $14 million or 4% from the previous year, primarily due to an 18-basis point increase in net interest margin.
  • Net Income (Loss) Attributable to SLM Corporation Common Stock: $132 million, compared to a net loss of $50 million in the same quarter last year, reflecting improved interest income and gains on loan sales.
  • Diluted Earnings (Loss) Per Common Share: $0.63, compared to a loss of $0.23 per share in the prior year, reflecting the turnaround in net income.
  • Gains (Losses) on Sales of Loans, Net: $136 million, resulting from a $1.94 billion Private Education Loan sale during the quarter, with no comparable gains in the prior year.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $179 million, decreased by $92 million or 34% from the previous year, primarily due to negative provisions from loan sales.
  • Total Non-Interest Income: $173 million, increased by $149 million from the previous year, driven by gains on loan sales and securities.
  • Total Non-Interest Expenses: $180 million, increased by $9 million or 5% from the previous year, primarily due to increased marketing spend.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's primary focus remains on maximizing the profitability and growth of its core private student loan business. The Private Education Loan portfolio is a significant driver of net interest income, with a notable increase in average Private Education Loans compared to the previous year.
  • Sales Units: For the three months ended September 30, 2025, the company originated $2.9 billion in Private Education Loans, an increase from $2.8 billion in the same period of 2024. The nine months ended September 30, 2025, saw total originations of $6.4 billion, up from $6.0 billion in the prior year.
  • Future Outlook: Management has provided guidance for 2025, focusing on sustaining credit quality and maintaining cost-efficient funding sources to support loan originations. The company is also committed to enhancing its risk management framework and assessing enterprise-wide risk.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is advancing its strategic imperatives by focusing on its core private student loan business, optimizing its brand, and maintaining a rigorous capital allocation program to create stockholder value.
  • Risk Management Enhancements: The company is strengthening its risk and compliance functions and enhancing its risk management framework to better manage enterprise-wide risk.
  • Loan Portfolio Management: The company sold $1.94 billion in Private Education Loans during the third quarter of 2025, which significantly impacted the provision for credit losses and resulted in gains on loan sales.

SEC Filing: SLM Corp [ SLM ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: MOODYS CORP /DE/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Moody's Corporation, a global leader in credit ratings, research, and risk analysis, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report highlights significant financial growth and robust operational performance across its key business segments, Moody's Analytics (MA) and Moody's Investors Service (MIS).

Financial Highlights

Revenue: $2,007 million, reflecting a year-over-year increase driven by growth in both MA and MIS segments.

Operating Income: $917 million, an improvement from the previous year, indicating enhanced operational efficiency.

Net Income: $647 million, up from $534 million in the prior year, showcasing strong profitability.

Net Income Attributable to Moody's: $646 million, demonstrating consistent performance with a focus on core business strengths.

Basic EPS: $3.61, compared to $2.94 in the previous year, highlighting improved earnings per share.

Diluted EPS: $3.60, up from $2.93, reflecting the company's ability to generate higher earnings for shareholders.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

  • Moody's Analytics (MA): Reported revenue from Decision Solutions, Research and Insights, and Data and Information. Decision Solutions revenue was driven by Banking, Insurance, and KYC services, with notable growth in Insurance and KYC. Research and Insights and Data and Information also contributed significantly to the MA segment's performance.
  • Moody's Investors Service (MIS): Saw revenue contributions from Corporate Finance, Structured Finance, Financial Institutions, and Public, Project and Infrastructure Finance. Corporate Finance was the largest contributor, with strong performance in investment-grade and high-yield sectors.

Geographical Performance

  • U.S. Market: Remains a significant contributor to Moody's revenue, with substantial contributions from both MA and MIS segments.
  • Non-U.S. Markets: Particularly EMEA and Asia-Pacific, showed strong performance, contributing to the overall growth in revenue.
  • EMEA Region: In the MA segment, EMEA was the largest non-U.S. contributor. In the MIS segment, EMEA also led the non-U.S. revenue.
  • Asia-Pacific Region: Showed robust growth in both MA and MIS segments.

Future Outlook

Moody's continues to focus on expanding its integrated risk assessment solutions and enhancing its product offerings across both MA and MIS segments. The company is also investing in strategic growth areas and operational efficiencies to drive future performance.

SEC Filing: MOODYS CORP /DE/ [ MCO ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: KAISER ALUMINUM CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Kaiser Aluminum Corp, a leading producer of semi-fabricated specialty aluminum products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by strategic pricing, cost management, and capacity expansions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $843.5 million, reflecting a 13% increase compared to the same quarter last year, driven by a 22% increase in the average realized sales price per pound.
  • Operating income: $48.8 million, a significant increase from $13.2 million in the prior year quarter, primarily due to improved pricing and cost management.
  • Net income: $39.5 million, up from $8.8 million in the previous year, reflecting strong operational performance and cost efficiencies.
  • Net income per common share, Diluted: $2.38, compared to $0.54 in the prior year, indicating improved profitability and earnings growth.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported a breakdown of net sales by end market applications for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, with Aero/HS Products contributing $182.2 million, Packaging $393.9 million, GE Products $192.2 million, and Automotive Extrusions $75.2 million. This reflects a significant increase in Packaging and GE Products sales compared to the previous year.
  • Sales Units: For the quarter ended September 30, 2025, the company shipped 270.2 million pounds of products, a decrease from 292.2 million pounds in the same period of 2024. This decline was primarily due to a planned partial outage at the Trentwood facility.
  • New Production Launches: The company completed the start-up of the fourth coating line at the Warrick facility, which is expected to enhance production capacity and efficiency.
  • Operational Performance: The company experienced a decrease in manufacturing costs by $49.4 million for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to favorable metal consumption and lower shipment volume.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued focus on strategic investments and capacity expansions, particularly in the Aero/HS Products and Packaging segments, to drive future growth and operational efficiency.

SEC Filing: KAISER ALUMINUM CORP [ KALU ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025

data.title: BOSTON BEER CO INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Boston Beer Co Inc, a leading player in the craft beer industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and future outlooks. Despite facing challenges in sales volume, the company has shown resilience through improved cost management and strategic operational efficiencies.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenue: $537.5 million, a decrease of 11.2% compared to the same period last year due to decreased sales volume, partially offset by increased pricing and favorable product mix.
  • Gross Profit: $273.1 million, representing a gross margin of 50.8%, with improvements driven by contract renegotiations, recipe optimization savings, and improved brewery efficiencies.
  • Operating Income: $62.0 million, increased by 35.4% from the prior year, reflecting lower operating expenses and improved cost management.
  • Net Income: $46.2 million, increased by 37.7% from the prior year, benefiting from lower cost of goods sold and reduced impairment charges.
  • Net Income per Common Share – Basic: $4.25, up from $2.87 in the prior year, reflecting improved profitability and cost efficiencies.

Business Highlights

  • Sales Units: The company experienced a 13.7% decrease in total shipment volume to 1,936,000 barrels for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, compared to 2,243,000 barrels in the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily due to reduced sales in Twisted Tea, Truly Hard Seltzer, and Samuel Adams brands, partially offset by growth in Sun Cruiser and Angry Orchard brands.
  • Sales Units: For the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025, total shipment volume decreased by 3.7% to 5,776,000 barrels, compared to 5,997,000 barrels in the same period in 2024, mainly due to declines in Twisted Tea and Truly Hard Seltzer brands.
  • Revenue Segments: Shipments to domestic distributors accounted for 93% of revenue for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, while international shipments contributed 5%, and sales at retail locations made up 2%.
  • Revenue Segments: For the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025, domestic shipments represented 94% of revenue, international shipments 5%, and retail sales 1%.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company achieved cost savings through contract renegotiations and recipe optimization, resulting in a decrease in cost of goods sold per barrel by 5.8% for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, and by 6.0% for the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025.
  • Advertising and Promotion: Advertising, promotional, and selling expenses increased by 11.3% for the thirteen weeks ended September 27, 2025, and by 12.0% for the thirty-nine weeks ended September 27, 2025, primarily due to increased brand media and local marketing investments.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates incurring approximately $31 million in shortfall fees related to production agreements, with $14 million expected to be expensed in the remainder of 2025 and $17 million in future years, primarily in 2026.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects tariffs to have an unfavorable cost impact for the full year 2025 of approximately $9 to $13 million, affecting gross margins by 40 to 60 basis points.

SEC Filing: BOSTON BEER CO INC [ SAM ] - 10-Q - Oct. 23, 2025
